reforms.description: The government is introducing a major reform to enhance the vocational education and training (VET) system, including a new dual VET programme that combines school and firm training with a high work-based component.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing the skills of the workforce through practical training is expected to improve employability and support innovation in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The new dual VET programme is set to be introduced in the autumn of 2014.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of the vocational education system, aiming to align training with labor market needs and improve the quality of human capital.
reforms.source_quote: In 2012 it introduced a new dual VET (simultaneous school and firm training) that has a high work-based component.

reforms.description: The government plans to publish information on labor market outcomes of graduates by degree and institution to enhance the relevance of university education.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing transparency on labor market outcomes, this reform aims to incentivize universities to specialize and improve the quality of education, thereby enhancing the employability of graduates.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is planning to publish information on labor market outcomes from 2014.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to improve the alignment of higher education with labor market needs, but it is not as comprehensive as a complete overhaul of the education system.
reforms.source_quote: The government is planning from 2014 to publish information on labour market outcomes of graduates by degree and institution.

reforms.description: The government aims to boost innovation capacity by expanding the use of performance-based funding for research institutions and enhancing collaboration with enterprises.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing high-performing research institutions, this reform is expected to enhance research quality and its impact on innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The central government should expand the use of performance-based funding.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift towards performance-based funding, which can fundamentally change how research institutions operate and collaborate with industry.
reforms.source_quote: The central government should expand the use of performance-based funding, such as the 'centre of research excellence' programme.

reforms.description: Spain is implementing a strategy to promote non-bank financial intermediation, focusing on SMEs and start-ups, which includes the establishment of a new securities market for medium-sized companies and the development of venture capital funding structures.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By diversifying financing sources, the reform aims to enhance access to capital for innovative firms, thereby fostering entrepreneurship and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy has been put into action with specific initiatives aimed at improving financing options for SMEs.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in the financing landscape for SMEs and start-ups, addressing critical gaps in access to capital and promoting innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Recent government initiatives...attempt to fill these gaps, with a special focus on SMEs and start-ups.

reforms.description: The government plans to create a national innovation agency to enhance the coordination and effectiveness of public funding for research and innovation, although this initiative has not yet been implemented.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The establishment of a national innovation agency is expected to improve the allocation of resources and coordination among various innovation actors, thereby enhancing overall innovation performance.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has plans to create a national innovation agency that would help achieve this but they have not been implemented yet.
reforms.importance_rationale: The creation of a national innovation agency represents a significant step towards improving the innovation ecosystem in Spain, although it is still in the planning phase.
reforms.source_quote: The government has plans to create a national innovation agency that would help achieve this but they have not been implemented yet.

reforms.description: The central government should increase block grant funding to universities and research centres to ensure long-term capability building in research, complementing competition-based funding.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding stability can enhance research capabilities and outputs, leading to better innovation outcomes.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has proposed increasing funding to support research institutions.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses a critical funding issue in the research ecosystem, aiming to improve the overall quality and sustainability of research outputs.
reforms.source_quote: The central government should ensure that universities and research centres receive a minimum share of block grant funding.

reforms.description: The government should streamline the R&D tax credit certification process to encourage more firms to utilize the credit effectively.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Simplifying the certification process can increase the uptake of R&D tax credits, thereby boosting private sector R&D investment.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has indicated plans to review and streamline the R&D tax credit process.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform aims to enhance the effectiveness of an existing instrument, potentially leading to increased R&D spending by firms.
reforms.source_quote: The authorities should streamline the certification process for R&D tax credits.

reforms.description: The central government is launching an industrial PhD programme aimed at enhancing knowledge transfer by allowing research staff to spend sabbatical periods in the enterprise sector, thereby fostering collaboration between academia and industry.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative is expected to enhance the skills of researchers and improve their engagement with the private sector, leading to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The central government is also launching an industrial PhD programme.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant step towards improving the link between academia and industry, which is crucial for enhancing innovation capacity in Spain.
reforms.source_quote: The central government is also launching an industrial PhD programme and it should work with the regions to spread best practice for industrial PhD programmes across Spain.

reforms.description: The Basque Country has developed a risk-sharing programme to reduce the financial risks for SMEs participating in innovation projects, thereby encouraging greater involvement in R&D activities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By lowering the financial barriers for SMEs, this programme is expected to stimulate innovation and enhance the overall competitiveness of the sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The Basque country has developed a risk-sharing programme, where SMEs take only a partial share in innovation projects.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it directly addresses the financial challenges faced by SMEs in engaging with innovation, which is critical for economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: To meet the challenge of increasing innovation among SMEs, the Basque country has developed a risk-sharing programme, where SMEs take only a partial share in innovation projects.

reforms.description: The Spanish government is planning reforms to university education aimed at improving efficiency and relevance to the labor market, including the introduction of a new accounting system to better identify costs and labor market outcomes for graduates.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing the relevance of university education to labor market needs, the reform aims to improve employability and productivity, thus supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is planning reforms to university education.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of the university education system, aiming to align educational outcomes with labor market demands, which is crucial for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The central government is also planning reforms to university education to improve efficiency in a system where 30% of degree programmes have fewer than 30 students.

reforms.description: The government plans to retain and review the R&D tax credit, including limits on the amounts that can be claimed, and cooperate with larger research organizations to promote its use among younger firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging R&D investment among younger firms can enhance innovation and productivity, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to retain and review the R&D tax credit.
reforms.importance_rationale: The review of the R&D tax credit is significant as it aims to enhance the effectiveness of an existing instrument that supports innovation among firms.
reforms.source_quote: Retain and review the research and development (R&D) tax credit including the limits on the amounts that can be claimed.

reforms.description: The Spanish government aims to deepen its efforts to expand the innovation system and encourage greater spillovers to the business sector, addressing the low rates of business R&D and innovation among SMEs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing the innovation system, the reform is expected to boost productivity and innovation rates among firms, particularly SMEs, which are crucial for economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government should deepen its efforts to expand the innovation system.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in policy focus towards enhancing the innovation ecosystem, which is essential for improving Spain's economic performance and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: The government should deepen its efforts to expand the innovation system and encourage greater spillovers to the business sector.

reforms.description: The government aims to raise the quality of innovation and strengthen competitiveness by encouraging greater scale and specialization of universities and research organizations, extending performance-based resource allocation, and providing more career opportunities for highly qualified researchers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing the quality of innovation and research capabilities is expected to lead to higher productivity and competitiveness in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has outlined plans to improve innovation quality and competitiveness.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in how Spain approaches innovation and research, aiming for systemic improvements in the education and research sectors.
reforms.source_quote: Raise the quality of innovation and strengthen competitiveness by encouraging greater scale and specialization of universities and research organisations.

reforms.description: The Spanish government has created the Council for Science, Technology and Innovation policies to improve the governance of the innovation system, aiming to expand research capabilities and linkages between science and industry.
reforms.package_name: Spanish Strategy for Science, Technology and Innovation
reforms.component_name: Council for Science, Technology and Innovation
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving governance of the innovation system is expected to enhance research capabilities and foster collaboration between science and industry, leading to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Council has been established to oversee innovation policies.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the governance of Spain's innovation system, which is crucial for enhancing the country's research and development capabilities.
reforms.source_quote: The Council for Science, Technology and Innovation policies has been created to improve the governance of the innovation system.

reforms.description: The Spanish government has introduced the Market Unity Law to simplify business licensing requirements and reduce administrative burdens for entrepreneurs, aiming to foster a more conducive environment for startups.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing barriers to entrepreneurship, the law is expected to stimulate business creation and expansion, thereby enhancing economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The law has been introduced to simplify business licensing requirements.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is a major step towards creating a more favorable regulatory environment for startups, which is essential for fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The government has introduced the Market Unity Law to simplify business licensing requirements.

reforms.description: The Spanish government plans to expand performance-based funding for research institutions to enhance their capabilities and attract more EU funding, particularly through the Horizon 2020 programme.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing funding based on performance, the initiative aims to improve research quality and output, which can lead to higher productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has outlined plans to introduce new incentives for research funding.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in funding strategy aimed at improving research capabilities and attracting external funding, which is crucial for enhancing Spain's innovation landscape.
reforms.source_quote: This could be done by introducing new incentives and developing new tools aimed at fostering aggregation of capabilities, networking and specialisation.

reforms.description: Spain has introduced the Entrepreneurs' Law (Law 14/2013) to simplify the process of starting a business by creating a network of 'Entrepreneur Support Points' to assist new entrepreneurs with regulatory procedures.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing barriers to entrepreneurship, the reform is expected to enhance business dynamism and job creation, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The law has been enacted and the support points established.
reforms.importance_rationale: This law represents a significant structural change aimed at fostering entrepreneurship, which is crucial for economic recovery and growth in Spain.
reforms.source_quote: The Entrepreneurs' Law foresees the creation of the so-called 'Entrepreneur Support Points' network.

reforms.description: The Spanish government announced a draft tax reform aimed at broadening the tax base, reducing tax credits, and lowering the standard corporate tax rate to 25% by 2016, while maintaining a reduced rate for startups introduced in 2013.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By simplifying the tax structure and reducing rates, the reform aims to incentivize firm growth and attract investment, which can enhance overall economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government announced a draft tax reform in June 2014.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of the corporate tax system, which is expected to have widespread implications for business operations and investment in Spain.
reforms.source_quote: The government announced in June 2014 a draft tax reform, which proposes broadening the base, reducing tax credits, reducing the standard tax rate to 25% by 2016 and suppressing the preferential rate for SMEs.

reforms.description: The Strategic Plan for the Internationalisation of the Spanish Economy includes measures for closer collaboration between the export promotion agency, ICEX, and the Centre for Technological and Industrial Development (CDTI) to enhance product innovation and export participation.
reforms.package_name: Strategic Plan for the Internationalisation of the Spanish Economy
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhances synergies between innovation and internationalisation, potentially boosting exports and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan includes 41 measures aimed at broadening the Spanish export base.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform aims to integrate innovation and export policies, which is significant for enhancing the competitiveness of Spanish SMEs.
reforms.source_quote: The Strategic Plan for the Internationalisation of the Spanish Economy includes measures for a closer collaboration between the export promotion agency, ICEX, and the Centre for Technological and Industrial Development (CDTI).

reforms.description: The government introduced a new out-of-court pre-insolvency payment procedure for SMEs, allowing for quicker debt restructuring and recovery.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitates faster recovery for SMEs, enabling them to continue operations and contribute to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The new procedure was introduced as part of the entrepreneurship law.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly alters the insolvency landscape for SMEs, promoting entrepreneurship and economic resilience.
reforms.source_quote: The government recently introduced new out-of-court pre-insolvency payment procedures for SMEs.

reforms.description: The government launched Fond-ICO Global, a public venture capital fund of funds aimed at promoting the creation of privately managed venture capital funds in Spain.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Aims to diversify funding sources for high-growth firms, enhancing their capacity to innovate and expand.
reforms.status_evidence: The fund was launched with a budget of EUR 1.2 billion.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant shift in the funding landscape for innovative firms in Spain.
reforms.source_quote: The government has launched a programme called Fond-ICO Global, the first public venture capital 'fund of funds' created in Spain.

reforms.description: Spain has established the Fond-ICO Global, a venture capital fund aimed at promoting the creation of privately managed venture capital funds that invest in Spanish companies at various stages of development, with a target to mobilize around EUR 5 billion.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By mobilizing private capital for investment in startups, the initiative is expected to enhance innovation and economic growth through increased funding for new ventures.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative seeks to promote the creation of privately managed venture capital funds.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of a significant venture capital fund represents a structural change in the financing landscape for startups in Spain, aiming to mobilize substantial private investment.
reforms.source_quote: Fond-ICO Global seeks to promote the creation of privately managed venture capital funds investing in Spanish companies at different stages of development.
